extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, which requires heart_failure AND hepatic_impairment AND renal_impairment AND qt_prolongation. rec_drug_b is set true only by r3, which requires heart_failure. These two rules write to different outputs, so conflict-resolution priorities never block one another. With heart_failure=hepatic_impairment=renal_impairment=qt_prolongation=true, r1 fires making rec_drug_a=true and r3 fires making rec_drug_b=true. The default false applies only to outputs left unset, so it does not help. Both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property. asthma and immunosuppressed only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set to false for a minimal counterexample.
